context: Transparency about the gate's conservatism is a first-class requirement. Every item not cleared must be recorded with its reason, and aggregate counts by exclusion reason published so external observers can verify the gate is not a black box. Depends on data-001. See PLAN.md (Success metrics, Solution approach section 8).
acceptanceCriteria: The quarantine ledger (ledger/quarantine.jsonl) contains a record for every item not cleared in the M1 run, each with a reason code.
An aggregate summary table is published alongside showing counts by exclusion reason (restricted, undetermined, needs-review, sensitive-hold).
The ledger is append-only and version-controlled so the history is auditable.
The ledger format is documented so external consumers can parse it.
The maintainer has reviewed the ledger before publication.
output: A committed quarantine ledger (ledger/quarantine.jsonl) and aggregate summary, reviewed and published.
title: Publish the quarantine ledger with reason counts
objective: Publish the quarantine ledger (ledger/quarantine.jsonl) populated from the M1 data run, with per-item reason records and an aggregate reason-count summary, making the gate's conservatism publicly transparent.
